italian-game.hint: It's time to challenge Black's center. What pawn break opens the position?
Develop a piece toward the center and target a weakness.

italian-game.question: What is Black's best strategic plan after White plays d4?
choices: Trade pawns with exd4 and develop quickly
Push e4 to gain space
Castle queenside for opposite-side attacks
Play a6 to prevent Bb5
italian-game.hint: After exd4 cxd4, Black can target d4 or play Bb4 to pin.

sicilian-najdorf.hint: Connect the queen to the dark-squared bishop for opposite-side castling.
Develop the bishop to a stable square before deciding on the plan.

sicilian-najdorf.question: What is White's main plan in the English Attack?
choices: Castle queenside and launch a kingside pawn storm with g4-g5
Castle kingside and play for a quiet positional game
Exchange queens and head for an endgame
Play Nd5 immediately to dominate the center
sicilian-najdorf.hint: The English Attack is all about opposite-side castling and attacking.
Pin the knight to create tactical pressure.

french-defence.hint: Which square puts the bishop on an active diagonal while keeping f4 available?
Get the king to safety before starting your counterplay.

french-defence.question: What is White's main plan in the French Advance?
choices: Support e5 with f4, develop pieces to active squares, and attack the kingside
Trade the e5 pawn and simplify to an endgame
Play c4 to attack d5 from both sides
Fianchetto the dark-squared bishop with g3-Bg2
french-defence.hint: White wants to maintain the space advantage and build a kingside attack.
Attack the base of White's pawn chain.

caro-kann.hint: Capture the pawn to simplify the center — this is the main idea of the Caro-Kann.
Support the d5 pawn and prepare to develop the dark-squared bishop.

caro-kann.question: What is Black's strategic plan after White plays d4 and Nf3?
choices: Develop with Nd7, e6, Ngf6, and aim for c5 to challenge the center
Push f5 to attack e4
Play Qa5+ and grab queenside pawns
Fianchetto with g6-Bg7 and castle kingside
caro-kann.hint: Solid development and the c5 break are key in the Caro-Kann.
Capture to enter the main line and develop the bishop.

ruy-lopez.hint: Maintain the pin while keeping options open. Don't exchange yet.
Prepare the central pawn break that defines the Closed Ruy Lopez.

ruy-lopez.question: What is White's main strategic plan in the Closed Ruy Lopez?
choices: Prepare d4 with c3 and build a strong center
Sacrifice on f7 for a quick attack
Exchange the bishop for the knight on c6
Play h4-h5 for a direct kingside assault
ruy-lopez.hint: The Closed Ruy is about slow central build-up, not quick attacks.
Protect e4 and prepare central action.

qgd.hint: Pin the knight and develop with purpose. This is the Classical QGD.
Challenge the d5 pawn directly to activate your pieces.

qgd.question: What is Black's best strategic approach in the QGD?
choices: Develop solidly with Be7, O-O, Nbd7 and wait for the right moment to break with c5 or e5
Attack immediately with c5
Play Bb4 to pin the knight
Capture on c4 and hold the pawn with b5
qgd.hint: The QGD is about solid development first, then choosing the right break.
Solidify the center before developing the bishop.

kings-indian-defence.hint: Launch the classic KID kingside counter-attack by challenging e4.
Support e5 and complete development before pushing pawns.

kings-indian-defence.question: What is White's best plan in the Classical King's Indian?
choices: Play d5 to close the center and expand on the queenside with c5
Exchange on e5 and simplify
Push f4 immediately to attack the kingside
Play Bg5 to pin the knight
kings-indian-defence.hint: When the center is closed, play on the wing where you have more space.
Complete development before committing to a plan.

london-system.hint: Challenge White's center immediately — the best reaction to the London.
Offer to trade the light-squared bishops. This is a key London idea.

london-system.question: What is Black's best approach against the London System?
choices: Challenge the center with c5 and develop actively
Mirror White's setup with Bf5 and e6
Play g6 and Bg7 for a KID-style setup
Push e5 immediately
london-system.hint: The most principled response is to fight for the center.
Develop the bishop to its best square eyeing h7.

english-opening.hint: Fianchetto the bishop to control the long diagonal.
Complete the fianchetto — the bishop belongs on g2.

english-opening.question: What is White's strategic plan in the English?
choices: Fianchetto the bishop with g3-Bg2 and control the center from the flank
Push d4 immediately to transpose to 1.d4 openings
Play f4 for a Grand Prix Attack
Develop Nf3-e4 for a direct center
english-opening.hint: The English is a flank opening — control the center indirectly.
Develop a piece and control the center.

sicilian-dragon.hint: Castle queenside to prepare the h4-h5 pawn storm on the kingside.
Continue the pawn storm — break open the h-file!

sicilian-dragon.question: What is White's main attacking plan in the Yugoslav Attack?
choices: Push h4-h5 to open the h-file against Black's king
Play Nd5 to dominate the center
Exchange dark-squared bishops and play a quiet game
Push f4-f5 to break through
sicilian-dragon.hint: In the Yugoslav Attack, the h-file pawn storm is the primary weapon.
Start queenside counterplay before White's kingside attack arrives.

scotch-game.hint: Capture the pawn that is attacking your center.
scotch-game.concept: Central pawn exchange in the Scotch Game

scotch-game.hint: Exchange the knight to double Black's pawns.
scotch-game.concept: Knight exchange to damage pawn structure

scotch-game.question: What is the best plan here?
choices: Develop pieces quickly with Nc3 and Bd3, exploiting the lead in development
Push e5 immediately to attack the knight
Castle queenside and launch a kingside pawn storm
Play a3 and b4 to expand on the queenside
scotch-game.hint: White has a development advantage after the pawn exchange — use it.
scotch-game.concept: Rapid development to exploit structural advantage

vienna-game.hint: Develop a piece that attacks White's e4 pawn.
vienna-game.concept: Counter-attacking the center in the Vienna Game

vienna-game.hint: Strike back in the center before White consolidates.
vienna-game.concept: Central counter-strike against the Vienna Gambit

vienna-game.question: What is the best plan here?
choices: Play fxe5 followed by d4 to seize the center
Play d3 to solidify the pawn chain and develop slowly
Castle queenside immediately
Play Bb5+ to disrupt Black's development
vienna-game.hint: Opening the center while you have a lead in development is powerful.
vienna-game.concept: Central pawn break to open lines in the Vienna Gambit

four-knights-game.hint: Pin the knight to the king, entering the Spanish variation.
Place the knight on a strong central square attacking the bishop.

four-knights-game.question: What is the best plan here?
choices: Play Nxe4 to win a pawn, exploiting the symmetry
Play d6 to solidify the center and develop the bishop
Play Bc5 to put pressure on f2
Play h6 to prevent Bg5
four-knights-game.hint: The symmetrical position allows a tactical trick involving the unprotected e4 pawn.
four-knights-game.concept: Tactical pawn win in the Italian Four Knights

sicilian-alapin.hint: Challenge the center immediately before White plays d4.
sicilian-alapin.concept: Central counter-strike against the Alapin Sicilian

sicilian-alapin.hint: Capture to open the center and free your pieces.
sicilian-alapin.concept: Pawn exchange to create open lines in the Alapin

sicilian-alapin.question: What is the best plan here?
choices: Play d4 to build a strong pawn center and develop naturally
Play Bb5+ to check the king immediately
Play Qa4+ to harass Black's king

sicilian-alapin.hint: Establishing a strong d4-c3 pawn duo is the key idea in the Alapin.
sicilian-alapin.concept: Building the ideal pawn center in the Alapin Sicilian

pirc-defence.hint: Develop the knight to pressure White's e4 pawn.

pirc-defence.hint: Support the e4 pawn and prepare a kingside attack.

pirc-defence.question: What is the best plan here?
choices: Castle kingside, play Be3, and prepare a central or queenside expansion
Push h4-h5 immediately to attack Black's king
Play e5 to blast open the center right away
Play a4-a5 to gain queenside space
pirc-defence.hint: Solid development and central control are more effective than premature attacks.
pirc-defence.concept: Classical setup against the Pirc Defence

scandinavian-defence.hint: Capture the pawn to challenge Black's center immediately.
scandinavian-defence.concept: Central pawn exchange in the Scandinavian

scandinavian-defence.hint: The queen needs a safe square that keeps pressure on the position.
scandinavian-defence.concept: Queen retreat to a5 in the main line Scandinavian

scandinavian-defence.question: What is the best plan for White here?
choices: Develop the bishop to d2 to connect the queen to the queenside and prepare long castle
Push a3 immediately to chase the queen
Play e4 to build a big pawn center
Fianchetto the kingside bishop with g3 and Bg2
scandinavian-defence.hint: White should develop efficiently while addressing the queen's placement on a5.

catalan-opening.hint: Stake a claim in the center before White completes the fianchetto.

Push b4 and a4 for an immediate queenside pawn storm
Play Nc3 and push e4 immediately on the next move
Trade queens with Qb3 and steer toward an endgame
catalan-opening.hint: The g2 bishop thrives on an open long diagonal.

slav-defence.hint: Develop the knight before committing to a pawn structure.

slav-defence.hint: Capture the pawn to open the diagonal for the light-squared bishop.
slav-defence.concept: Pawn capture to develop the c8 bishop in the Slav

slav-defence.question: What is White's best plan against the Slav with Bf5?
choices: Play Qb3 to attack b7 and pressure the queenside
Push e3 and Bd3 to trade off Black's strong bishop
Play g4 immediately to chase the bishop

slav-defence.hint: Targeting a key pawn creates immediate pressure.

nimzo-indian.hint: Solid development that supports d4 and prepares to develop the kingside.

nimzo-indian.hint: Get the king to safety before committing to a pawn structure.

nimzo-indian.question: What is White's best strategic plan here?
choices: Play Bd3 and aim for e4 to seize the center and gain the bishop pair

grunfeld-defence.hint: Capture to build a strong pawn center.

grunfeld-defence.hint: Exchange the knight and damage White's pawn structure.

grunfeld-defence.question: What is Black's best plan against White's big center?

Play a6 and b5 for a queenside minority attack
Play Nc6 and Bg4 to put pressure on d4 indirectly
